aliases: Hai Kah Lang White Coffee Cold

english: Hai Kah Lang White Coffee Cold

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn term for Malaysian/Singaporean white coffee exists; closest parent 'coffee beverage' (FOODON_03301638) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN0KL (Coffee, with milk) is the closest parent; no specific code for Southeast Asian white coffee with sweetened condensed milk exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34731007 (Coffee substance) is the closest available anchor; no specific code for white coffee beverage preparation exists.

main_ingredients: white coffee (low-roast Robusta or Arabica beans roasted with palm oil margarine)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of cold white coffee served at a Singaporean kopitiam or café, approximately 300 ml, typically containing brewed white coffee mixed with sweetened condensed milk and served over ice.

nutrient_source.title: Coffee, with milk and sugar (kopi) — adapted for white coffee with sweetened condensed milk; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central entry for coffee beverage with milk and sugar
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA FoodData Central (cross-reference)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Singapore Foodo lists kopi (coffee with condensed milk and sugar) as the closest Singaporean analogue. White coffee differs in that the beans are roasted with palm oil margarine at lower temperature, yielding a slightly lighter, less bitter, and more aromatic brew. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB kopi data and cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central NDB 14209 (Coffee, brewed, with milk and sugar) to produce per-100g estimates for a standard white coffee with condensed milk preparation. Serving size set at 300 g (approximately 300 ml). Values represent the ready-to-drink beverage including condensed milk.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sweetened condensed milk and coffee beverages with added sugar — analogous estimate based on sucrose GI and condensed milk contribution
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Malaysian/Singaporean white coffee with sweetened condensed milk. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened condensed milk (GI ~61) and the diluting effect of brewed coffee (GI effectively 0 for the coffee fraction). The dominant glycemic driver is the sweetened condensed milk (sucrose and lactose). A blended GI of approximately 55 is defensible given the condensed milk proportion in a standard serving. Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (55 × 28.5 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 15.7.

hypertension.guidance: A 300 ml serving provides approximately 84 mg sodium; caffeine in coffee can transiently raise blood pressure, so individuals with hypertension should limit intake to one cup per day and prefer unsweetened or reduced-condensed-milk preparations.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High added sugar from sweetened condensed milk
type2_diabetes.guidance: A standard 300 ml serving contains approximately 27 g of sugar predominantly from sweetened condensed milk, producing a glycemic load of ~15.7, which is significant for blood glucose management; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should request reduced or no condensed milk and monitor postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from condensed milk and palm oil margarine roasting
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each 300 ml serving contributes approximately 2.1 g saturated fat from condensed milk and residual palm oil margarine used in the white coffee roasting process; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency of consumption and choose lower-fat milk alternatives where available.

llm_training.natural_language_description: HKL White Coffee (Cold) — also known as Hai Kah Lang White Coffee — is a cold-served version of the traditional Malaysian-style white coffee (白咖啡), a specialty beverage originating from Ipoh, Perak, Malaysia, and widely popular in Singapore. Unlike conventional dark-roasted kopitiam coffee, white coffee is made from coffee beans roasted at a lower temperature with palm oil margarine (and sometimes wheat flour), which produces a lighter, less bitter, and more aromatic brew with a characteristic pale golden-brown colour. The term 'white' refers to the roasting method rather than the colour of the final drink. The cold version is prepared by brewing the white coffee concentrate and mixing it with sweetened condensed milk, then serving it over ice. The result is a creamy, mildly sweet, smooth beverage with a gentle coffee bitterness and a rich dairy note from the condensed milk. In Singapore, white coffee is commonly served at kopitiams (traditional coffee shops) and modern café chains. Nutritionally, a standard 300 ml cold serving provides approximately 156 kcal, with the primary energy contribution coming from carbohydrates (~28.5 g) and fat (~3.3 g), largely driven by the sweetened condensed milk. The sugar content (~27 g per serving) is clinically significant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. The beverage contains moderate amounts of calcium (~126 mg per serving) from the dairy component. Caffeine content is present from the coffee fraction and may affect individuals sensitive to stimulants or those managing hypertension. This beverage is halal-certified in most Singapore establishments and is suitable for vegetarians but not vegans due to the dairy content.
common_user_queries: How many calories in white coffee cold?
Is white coffee good for diabetics?
What is the difference between white coffee and regular kopi?
How much sugar is in cold white coffee?
Is white coffee high in sugar?
Can I drink white coffee if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry exists for Malaysian/Singaporean white coffee with condensed milk served cold. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB kopi (coffee with condensed milk) data and cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central coffee-with-milk-and-sugar entries. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using sweetened condensed milk as the primary glycemic driver. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are per 100 g of the ready-to-drink cold white coffee beverage (including condensed milk and ice melt contribution). Serving size is 300 g (~300 ml). 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA cross-reference for this beverage category; no industrial trans fats are expected in this preparation. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a liquid beverage with no fibrous plant material retained after brewing. 4. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero; coffee beverages with condensed milk contain negligible vitamin C. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB or USDA source databases for this beverage category. 6. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB or USDA source databases for this beverage category. 7. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB or USDA source databases for this beverage category. 8. GI set to 55 (low) as an analogous estimate; glycemic load per 300 g serving = 15.7. 9. The food_name in the input ('HKL White Coffee 海脚人白咖啡 (Cold/冷)') has been normalised to the canonical English form 'White Coffee (Cold)'; the brand/restaurant name 'Hai Kah Lang' and Chinese characters are preserved in aliases. 10. Halal status is assumed based on standard kopitiam white coffee preparation; individual restaurant certification should be verified. 11. glycemic_index set to 55 (low) based on analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database using sweetened condensed milk GI data as primary glycemic driver.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Drafted full record. Identified that dietary_fibre_g=0.0 and vitamin_c_mg=0.0 required zero-justification phrases in notes — added. Verified all aliases_by_language entries present in flat aliases list.

Iteration 2: Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' for glycemic_index 55, aligning with University of Sydney GI Database classification (GI <= 55 is low). Updated gi_source.source_match_notes and notes fields for consistency. Verified all -1 sentinel values documented in notes (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg). Confirmed GI numeric > 0 (55). Confirmed ontology match_type values use only allowed strings (exact/closest_parent/none). Confirmed trans_fat_g=0.0 documented as confirmed zero. All hard gates and Q1-Q8 now pass.
